justification: All six required schema sections present (integration_assessment, observability_review, resilience_assessment, operational_readiness, escalations, confidence). Logging assessed as adequate, metrics assessed as adequate, health check confirmed covering DB and broker dependencies. Resilience patterns confirmed: retry with exponential backoff on broker, circuit breaker on email provider, 5s timeouts on outbound HTTP. Rollback confirmed clean with no migrations and previous image available. Verdict is READY WITH CAVEATS with clear rationale. No fabricated gaps — all noted issues (DLQ, graceful degradation, runbook, tracing) are genuine observations about undefined behavior, not invented missing items. Distributed tracing correctly treated as a nice-to-have/escalation rather than a blocker.

observations.text: All six output schema sections present and well-structured with YAML blocks
Logging, metrics, and health check each individually assessed and confirmed adequate
Retry with backoff, circuit breaker, and timeouts each explicitly confirmed present
Rollback plan thoroughly assessed — no migrations, clean image rollback, even noted forward compatibility consideration
Verdict is READY WITH CAVEATS with detailed rationale distinguishing must-haves from nice-to-haves
Distributed tracing correctly categorized as nice-to-have, not a blocker
Escalations are non-blocking observations routed to appropriate team members

Some caveats (DLQ, graceful degradation, on-call runbook) are elevated to 'must-haves before production' which could be read as soft-blocking the READY verdict, though the final verdict still says READY WITH CAVEATS
Resilience assessment introduces scenarios not in the original scenario (poison messages, burst traffic, pool exhaustion) — thorough but edges toward inventing gaps that weren't present in the input
Confidence section clearly separates high-confidence from low-confidence areas with explicit assumptions listed
